implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Main UI component that manages the state and orchestrates all task operations (add, edit, delete, mark as completed/pending) by calling API methods and rendering TaskForm and TaskItem components.
implementation.name: App
implementation.pseudo_code:
On mount:
    Call loadTasks to fetch all tasks from backend
On '+ Add Task' click:
    Open TaskForm for new task
On TaskForm submit:
    If editingTask is null:
        Call handleCreateTask (calls api.createTask)
    Else:
        Call handleUpdateTask (calls api.updateTask)
On delete button:
    Call handleDeleteTask (calls api.deleteTask)
On complete/pending toggle:
    Call handleToggleComplete (calls api.updateTask with toggled completed)
Render:
    TaskForm (for add/edit)
    List of TaskItem components for each task

missing_implementations: A new task must include a tag (the UI uses 'category' as tag, but it is not required and can be empty; the backend does not enforce presence of tag/category).

implementation.name: read_tasks
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: API endpoint to list all tasks (events). Called to display existing events to the user.
implementation.pseudo_code:
Define GET /tasks endpoint
  Call get_tasks() from database
  Return list of tasks

implementation.name: create_task

  Return created task

implementation.name: update_task
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: API endpoint to edit an existing task (event). Called when a user submits the edit event form.

Define PUT /tasks/{task_id} endpoint
  Accept TaskCreate payload
  Call update_task() in database
  If not found, raise 404
  Return updated task

  Call delete_task() in database
  If not found, raise error
  Return success

implementation.name: add_task

Create new Task object with unique id and provided fields
Append to tasks list
Save tasks
Return new task

Find task by id
Replace with updated Task object

Return updated task or None

Remove task with matching id
Save tasks
Return True if deleted, False otherwise

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: UI form for adding and editing events. Collects user input and calls backend endpoints.

Display form fields for title, description, priority, category, due date
On submit, call onSubmit with form data
On cancel, call onCancel

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: UI component for displaying, editing, marking complete, and deleting events.
implementation.pseudo_code:
Display event details (title, description, priority, category, due date)
Show buttons for edit, delete, mark complete
Call appropriate handlers on button click

missing_implementations: A new event must include a location (no location field in backend or frontend).
A new event must include a description (description is optional, not required).
Ability to mark events as archived (no archive field or logic in backend or frontend).
Event uses 'title' instead of 'name' (minor naming mismatch).
Multiple tasks can be assigned to a single event.

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Provides the UI button for toggling a task's status between 'Completed' and 'Pending'. Visually distinguishes completed tasks (opacity, line-through, button label). Calls the handler to update status.
implementation.name: TaskItem component (Toggle Complete Button)
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF user clicks the 'Done' or 'Undo' button:
    CALL onToggleComplete(task)
Display task with:
    IF task.completed:
        Apply opacity and line-through style
        Show 'Undo' button
    ELSE:
        Show 'Done' button

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Handles toggling the task status in the UI. Invoked by TaskItem, updates the task's completed status via API, reloads tasks to reflect changes instantly.
implementation.name: handleToggleComplete
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION handleToggleComplete(task):
    TRY:
        CALL api.updateTask(task.id, { ...task, completed: !task.completed })
        CALL loadTasks() to refresh UI
    CATCH error:
        Show error alert

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Allows users to review tasks by status ('Completed', 'Pending', 'All'). Filters tasks in the UI based on their completed state.
implementation.name: filterStatus logic
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF filterStatus == 'completed':
    Show only tasks where task.completed == true
ELSE IF filterStatus == 'pending':
    Show only tasks where task.completed == false
ELSE:
    Show all tasks

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Sends updated task status to backend via PUT request. Ensures status change is persisted in storage.
implementation.name: api.updateTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION updateTask(id, task):
    SEND PUT request to /tasks/{id} with updated task data
    IF response OK:
        RETURN updated task
    ELSE:
        THROW error

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Receives task update requests from frontend. Calls database logic to persist status change.
implementation.name: update_task endpoint
implementation.pseudo_code:
ENDPOINT PUT /tasks/{task_id}:
    CALL database.update_task(task_id, task)
    IF updated_task is None:
        RETURN 404 error
    ELSE:
        RETURN updated_task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Updates the task's completed status (and other fields) in persistent storage. Saves changes to tasks.json.
implementation.name: database.update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION update_task(task_id, task_update):
    FOR each task in tasks:
        IF task.id == task_id:
            updated_task = Task(id=task_id, **task_update.model_dump())
            Replace task with updated_task
            CALL save_tasks(tasks)
            RETURN updated_task
    RETURN None

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Displays each task and provides an 'Edit' button. When clicked, triggers the edit flow by calling onEdit with the current task.
implementation.name: TaskItem (Edit Button)
implementation.pseudo_code:
FOR each task in the list:
    DISPLAY task details (title, description, priority, due date)
    DISPLAY 'Edit' button
    WHEN 'Edit' button is clicked:
        CALL onEdit(task)

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Renders a form for editing task fields (title, description, priority, due date). Pre-fills fields with existing values. On submit, calls onSubmit with updated values.

IF initialTask is provided:
    SET form fields to initialTask values
    DISPLAY form with fields: title, description, priority, due date
    WHEN user modifies fields and submits:
        CALL onSubmit(updatedTask)

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Receives PUT requests to /tasks/{task_id}, calls database.update_task to modify the task, and returns the updated task.
implementation.name: update_task (FastAPI endpoint)
implementation.pseudo_code:
@app.put('/tasks/{task_id}')
FUNCTION update_task(task_id, task):
    CALL database.update_task(task_id, task)
    IF updated_task is None:
        RAISE 404 error
    RETURN updated_task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Updates the task in the database file, preserving the original task ID and replacing fields with new values. Saves changes and returns the updated task.
implementation.name: update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION update_task(task_id, task_update):
    LOAD all tasks
    FOR each task in tasks:
        IF task.id == task_id:
            CREATE updated_task with task_id and new values
            REPLACE task with updated_task
            SAVE tasks
            RETURN updated_task
    RETURN None

missing_implementations: Input validation for title, description, priority, and due date to ensure data integrity (e.g., title required, valid priority values, due date format).
Instant update propagation across all views (e.g., real-time updates or state synchronization if multiple clients are connected).

Display a form with fields: title, description, priority, category, due date
On form submit:

IF response is not OK

RETURN updated task from response

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Defines the backend API endpoint for updating a task. Receives the updated task data, calls the database update function, and returns the updated task.

ON PUT /tasks/{task_id}
    CALL database.update_task(task_id, task)
    IF updated_task is None
        RAISE 404 error
    RETURN updated_task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Implements the database logic for updating a task. Finds the task by ID, replaces its details with the new values, and saves the updated list to disk.
implementation.name: update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
LOAD tasks from storage
FOR each task in tasks
    IF task.id == task_id
        CREATE updated_task with new values, retaining task_id
        REPLACE task with updated_task
        SAVE tasks to storage
        RETURN updated_task
RETURN None if not found

missing_implementations: Validation for data integrity (e.g., checking for valid title, due date, priority) is not explicitly implemented in frontend or backend.
Instant reflection of edits in UI is dependent on parent component logic, which is not shown here (e.g., App.tsx).

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Renders the UI for toggling task completion status. Displays a 'Done' button for pending tasks and 'Undo' button for completed tasks. When clicked, calls the onToggleComplete callback handler. Visually indicates task completion state through strikethrough text decoration and reduced opacity.
implementation.name: TaskItem
implementation.pseudo_code:
COMPONENT TaskItem:
  INPUT: task object with completed boolean property
  RENDER:
    IF task.completed == true:
      SET text-decoration to line-through
      SET opacity to 0.7
      SET button text to 'Undo'
      SET button class to 'btn-ghost'
    ELSE:
      SET button text to 'Done'
      SET button class to 'btn-primary'
    BUTTON onClick:
      CALL onToggleComplete(task)
  END_COMPONENT

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Core handler function that manages task status updates on the frontend. Receives the task object, toggles its completed boolean value (from true to false or vice versa), and sends an API request to update the task. After successful update, reloads all tasks to reflect the change in the UI.
implementation.name: handleToggleComplete
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION handleToggleComplete(task):
  TRY:
    new_completed_status = NOT task.completed
    CALL api.updateTask(task.id, {...task, completed: new_completed_status})
    CALL loadTasks() # Reload all tasks from backend
  CATCH error:
    ALERT 'Failed to update task status'
  END_TRY
END_FUNCTION

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: API client function that sends a PUT request to the backend with the updated task data including the new completed status. Handles the HTTP request/response cycle and throws an error if the request fails.
implementation.name: updateTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION updateTask(id, task_data):
  HTTP_REQUEST:
    METHOD: PUT
    URL: http://localhost:8000/tasks/{id}
    HEADERS: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
    BODY: JSON.stringify(task_data) # Includes completed: boolean
  IF response.ok:
    RETURN response.json() # Returns updated Task object
  ELSE:
    THROW Error('Failed to update task')
  END_IF
END_FUNCTION

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: FastAPI endpoint that receives PUT requests to /tasks/{task_id}. Accepts TaskCreate object with the updated completed status, delegates to database.update_task for persistence, and returns the updated Task object or 404 if task not found.
implementation.name: update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
ENDPOINT PUT /tasks/{task_id}:
  INPUT: task_id (string), task (TaskCreate object with completed field)
  CALL updated_task = database.update_task(task_id, task)
  IF updated_task is None:
    RAISE HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Task not found')
  ELSE:
    RETURN updated_task # Response model: Task
  END_IF
END_ENDPOINT

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Business logic function that handles the task status update. Loads all tasks from JSON storage, finds the task by ID, creates a new Task object with updated properties (including the new completed status), replaces the old task in the list, and persists the changes to the database file.
implementation.name: update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION update_task(task_id, task_update):
  tasks = get_tasks() # Load all tasks from tasks.json
  FOR each task in tasks:
    IF task.id == task_id:
      new_task = Task(id=task_id, **task_update fields)
      # new_task.completed is set from task_update
      tasks[current_index] = new_task
      save_tasks(tasks) # Persist to tasks.json
      RETURN new_task
    END_IF
  END_FOR
  RETURN None # Task not found
END_FUNCTION

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Persists the updated task list to the tasks.json database file. Converts Task objects to JSON format using Pydantic's model_dump method and writes to disk, ensuring the completed status is properly serialized.
implementation.name: save_tasks
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION save_tasks(tasks):
  OPEN file tasks.json in write mode:
    json_data = []
    FOR each task in tasks:
      # Convert Task object to JSON-compatible dictionary
      json_dict = task.model_dump(mode='json')
      # json_dict includes completed: boolean
      APPEND json_dict to json_data
    END_FOR
    WRITE json_data to file with indent=4
  CLOSE file
END_FUNCTION

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: This function is triggered when user clicks the Edit button on a task item. It sets the editingTask state to the selected task and opens the form modal, enabling edit mode.
implementation.name: openEdit

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Renders individual task items with an Edit button. When Edit button is clicked, it calls onEdit callback with the task object, triggering the openEdit function in App component.
implementation.name: TaskItem

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Displays form for editing task. When initialTask prop is provided (set by App component), it pre-populates all form fields with existing task data. The title input has HTML5 required attribute to prevent empty submissions. On form submission, calls onSubmit callback with updated task data.
implementation.name: TaskForm
implementation.pseudo_code:
COMPONENT TaskForm(initialTask, onSubmit) {
  IF initialTask exists THEN
    SET title = initialTask.title
    SET description = initialTask.description
    SET priority = initialTask.priority
    SET category = initialTask.category
    SET dueDate = initialTask.due_date
    DISPLAY 'Edit Task' heading
  ELSE
    DISPLAY 'Add New Task' heading
  END IF
  
  ADD REQUIRED attribute to title input field
  
  ON form submit:
    CALL onSubmit with form data {
      title, description, priority, category, due_date, completed
    }
}

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Receives form submission from TaskForm component. Validates that editingTask exists, then calls the updateTask API endpoint with the task ID and updated data. Upon success, reloads tasks, closes form, and clears editingTask state.
implementation.name: handleUpdateTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
ASYNC FUNCTION handleUpdateTask(taskData: TaskCreate) {
  IF editingTask is null THEN
    RETURN
  END IF
  
  TRY
    CALL api.updateTask(editingTask.id, taskData)
    CALL loadTasks()  // Refresh task list from server
    SET editingTask = null
    SET isFormOpen = false
  CATCH error
    DISPLAY alert: 'Failed to update task'
  END TRY
}

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Makes HTTP PUT request to backend endpoint with task ID and updated task data. Sends request to localhost:8000/tasks/{id} with updated fields in request body.
implementation.name: updateTask

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: FastAPI endpoint that receives PUT requests at /tasks/{task_id}. Extracts task_id from URL path and task update data from request body, then calls database.update_task function to persist changes.
implementation.name: update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
ASYNC ENDPOINT PUT /tasks/{task_id} {
  RECEIVE task_id: string from URL
  RECEIVE task: TaskCreate from request body
  
  CALL updated_task = database.update_task(task_id, task)
  
  IF updated_task is null THEN
    RAISE HTTPException(404, 'Task not found')
  END IF
  
  RETURN updated_task as JSON
}

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Core business logic for updating a task. Retrieves all tasks from database, finds task by ID, replaces it with updated data, saves to JSON file, and returns the updated task.
implementation.name: update_task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Persists updated task data to JSON file. Converts Task objects to JSON-compatible format and writes to tasks.json file for persistent storage.
implementation.name: save_tasks
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION save_tasks(tasks: List[Task]) {
  OPEN tasks.json file in write mode
  
  CONVERT tasks to JSON format:
    FOR EACH task in tasks:
      json_data.append(task.model_dump(mode='json'))
  
  WRITE json_data to file with indentation
  CLOSE file
}

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Validates that edited task title is not empty before persisting changes. Validates the TaskCreate model containing the edited task data to ensure title field is non-empty before the task is saved to database. This is implemented as a Pydantic field validator on the title field in the TaskBase model.
implementation.name: validate_title

missing_implementations: Backend validation to prevent saving an edited task with an empty or whitespace-only title. The TaskCreate model needs a field validator to ensure the title is not empty before the updated task is persisted.

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: React component that renders the form UI for creating a new task. It collects input from the user for title, description, priority, category, and due_date fields. The component accepts an onSubmit callback that is triggered when the user clicks the Save Task button. On form submission, it validates required fields (title is required) and passes the form data to the parent component.
implementation.name: TaskForm Component
implementation.pseudo_code:
COMPONENT TaskForm(props)
  INITIALIZE state variables:
    title = initialTask?.title OR empty string
    description = initialTask?.description OR empty string
    priority = initialTask?.priority OR 'Medium'
    category = initialTask?.category OR empty string
    dueDate = initialTask?.due_date OR empty string
  
  FUNCTION handleSubmit(event):
    PREVENT default form submission
    CREATE taskData object:
      taskData.title = title
      taskData.description = description IF description is not empty ELSE undefined
      taskData.priority = priority
      taskData.category = category IF category is not empty string ELSE undefined
      taskData.due_date = dueDate IF dueDate is not empty ELSE undefined
      taskData.completed = initialTask?.completed OR false
    
    AWAIT onSubmit(taskData)
  
  RENDER form with:
    - Title label and required text input field
    - Description label and textarea field
    - Priority dropdown (Low, Medium, High)
    - Category dropdown (None, Work, Personal, Study)
    - Due Date input field (type: date)
    - Cancel button (onClick: onCancel)
    - Save Task button (type: submit, triggers handleSubmit)
  
  RETURN form JSX

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Main React component that manages the application state and orchestrates the task creation flow. It handles the opening/closing of the form modal, calls the API to create tasks, and refreshes the task list after creation. It also manages other states like filtering and sorting of tasks.
implementation.name: App Container Component
implementation.pseudo_code:
COMPONENT App
  INITIALIZE state:
    tasks = empty array
    isFormOpen = false
    editingTask = null
    isLoading = true
    filterStatus = 'all'
    sortOption = 'dueDate'
  
  ON component mount:
    CALL loadTasks()
  
  FUNCTION loadTasks():
    TRY:
      data = AWAIT api.getTasks()
      SET tasks = data
    CATCH error:
      LOG error to console
    FINALLY:
      SET isLoading = false
  
  FUNCTION handleCreateTask(taskData):
    TRY:
      AWAIT api.createTask(taskData)
      AWAIT loadTasks()  // Refresh task list
      SET isFormOpen = false
    CATCH error:
      SHOW alert 'Failed to create task'
  
  FUNCTION openFormForCreate():
    SET editingTask = null
    SET isFormOpen = true
  
  FUNCTION openFormForEdit(task):
    SET editingTask = task
    SET isFormOpen = true
  
  FUNCTION closeForm():
    SET isFormOpen = false
    SET editingTask = null
  
  RENDER:
    - Header with 'My Tasks' title and '+ Add Task' button
      - ONCLICK '+ Add Task': openFormForCreate()
    - IF isFormOpen:
      - RENDER modal overlay
      - RENDER TaskForm component:
        - initialTask = editingTask
        - onSubmit = IF editingTask EXISTS THEN handleUpdateTask ELSE handleCreateTask
        - onCancel = closeForm()
    - RENDER filter and sort controls
    - APPLY filters and sorting to tasks array
    - RENDER TaskItem components for each filtered task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Frontend API client function that makes an HTTP POST request to the backend endpoint. It sends the task data as JSON in the request body. It validates that the response is successful and returns the created task object with the generated ID from the server.
implementation.name: createTask API Method
implementation.pseudo_code:
ASYNC FUNCTION createTask(task: TaskCreate) -> Task:
  CONST API_URL = 'http://localhost:8000'
  
  CONST response = AWAIT fetch(API_URL + '/tasks', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify(task)
  })
  
  IF NOT response.ok:
    THROW Error('Failed to create task')
  
  RETURN response.json()  // Returns Task object with generated ID

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: FastAPI HTTP endpoint that receives POST requests at /tasks. It accepts a TaskCreate model from the request body, passes it to the database layer's add_task function, and returns the created Task object with the generated ID. The endpoint enforces Pydantic model validation on the incoming request.
implementation.name: create_task Endpoint
implementation.pseudo_code:
ENDPOINT POST /tasks
  INPUT: task (TaskCreate model)
  
  CALL database.add_task(task)
  
  RESPONSE:
    - Status: 200 OK
    - Body: Task model (includes generated ID)
    - Content-Type: application/json

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Database layer function that implements the core business logic for creating a new task. It generates a unique UUID for the task ID, creates a Task object, appends it to the in-memory task list, persists all tasks to the JSON file, and returns the newly created task with its ID.
implementation.name: add_task Function

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Persistence layer function that serializes the task list to JSON format and writes it to the tasks.json file. It is called after add_task to ensure the newly created task is persisted. Uses Pydantic's model_dump with mode='json' to handle date serialization.
implementation.name: save_tasks Function
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION save_tasks(tasks: List[Task]) -> void:
  OPEN file 'tasks.json' in write mode:
    // Convert each Task object to JSON-serializable dictionary
    json_data = []
    FOR EACH task IN tasks:
      json_dict = task.model_dump(mode='json')
      json_data.append(json_dict)
    
    // Write JSON array to file with indentation
    json.dump(json_data, file, indent=4)
    
    CLOSE file
